title: Hasty Pudding Theatricals

infobox.value: This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. is a student theatrical society at [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University") known for its annual [burlesque](/wiki/Burlesque "Burlesque") [crossdressing](/wiki/Crossdressing "Crossdressing") [musicals](/wiki/Musical_theatre "Musical theatre") as well as its [Man](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Man_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Man of the Year") and [Woman](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Woman_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Woman of the Year") of the Year awards.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The Pudding is the oldest theatrical organization in the United States and the third oldest in the world.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Its annual production is a musical comedy that often touches on topical social and political issues.

Yano, Christine R.; Akatsuka, Neal K. Adolph (August 9, 2018). [*Straight A's: Asian American College Students in Their Own Words*](https://books.google.com/books?id=OVxnDwAAQBAJ&dq=hasty+pudding+burlesque+crossdressing&pg=PT121). Duke University Press. [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [42°22′21.2″N 71°7′5.2″W﻿ / ﻿42.372556°N 71.118111°W﻿ / 42.372556; -71.118111](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&params=42_22_21.2_N_71_7_5.2_W_region:US)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Student theatrical society at Harvard

For other uses, see [Hasty pudding (disambiguation)](/wiki/Hasty_pudding_(disambiguation) "Hasty pudding (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dd/The_Hasty_Pudding_Lobby%2C_12_Holyoke_St%2C_Cambridge_MA.jpg/250px-The_Hasty_Pudding_Lobby%2C_12_Holyoke_St%2C_Cambridge_MA.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Hasty_Pudding_Lobby,_12_Holyoke_St,_Cambridge_MA.jpg)

The Hasty Pudding lobby

**Hasty Pudding Theatricals** is a student theatrical society at [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University") known for its annual [burlesque](/wiki/Burlesque "Burlesque") [crossdressing](/wiki/Crossdressing "Crossdressing") [musicals](/wiki/Musical_theatre "Musical theatre") as well as its [Man](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Man_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Man of the Year") and [Woman](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Woman_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Woman of the Year") of the Year awards.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The Pudding is the oldest theatrical organization in the United States and the third oldest in the world.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Its annual production is a musical comedy that often touches on topical social and political issues.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Hasty Pudding Theatricals was formed in 1795 as a [social club](/wiki/Social_club "Social club") for [Harvard College](/wiki/Harvard_College "Harvard College") students to cultivate the social affections and cherish the feelings of friendship and patriotism. On December 13, 1844, the Pudding put on its first full performance – William Barnes Rhodes' burlesque opera *[Bombastes Furioso](/wiki/Bombastes_Furioso "Bombastes Furioso").* After a period of producing popular comedies by established playwrights, student members of the Pudding began to write their own shows, starting in 1882 with a production of *Dido and Aeneas* by [Owen Wister](/wiki/Owen_Wister "Owen Wister").[[3]](#cite_note-history-3) The event was interrupted for 2 years during each of the [World Wars](/wiki/World_war "World war") and once more in 2020 due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").

The cast was limited to only men (often performing in drag) until 2018,[[4]](#cite_note-4) though women played a role in the company for many years, primarily as [costumers](/wiki/Costume_designer "Costume designer"). In 1948 [Felisa Vanoff](/wiki/Felisa_Vanoff "Felisa Vanoff") became Pudding's first female choreographer,[[5]](#cite_note-auto-5)[[6]](#cite_note-auto1-6) and in 1978 Diane Nabatoff became its first female producer. In 2009 [Megan Amram](/wiki/Megan_Amram "Megan Amram") and [Alexandra Petri](/wiki/Alexandra_Petri "Alexandra Petri") became the first all-female team to write the musical comedy. The technical crew, band, and business staff has been co-ed for many years.

## Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

* [BJ Averell](/wiki/BJ_Averell "BJ Averell"), Grand Prize winner, *[The Amazing Race](/wiki/The_Amazing_Race "The Amazing Race")*
* [Megan Amram](/wiki/Megan_Amram "Megan Amram")
* [Paris Barclay](/wiki/Paris_Barclay "Paris Barclay"), Emmy-winning director and producer for dozens of film and television projects including *[NYPD Blue](/wiki/NYPD_Blue "NYPD Blue")*, *[Sons of Anarchy](/wiki/Sons_of_Anarchy "Sons of Anarchy")* and [*Glee*](/wiki/Glee_(TV_series) "Glee (TV series)"), and in 2013 was elected President of the [Directors Guild of America](/wiki/Directors_Guild_of_America "Directors Guild of America")
* [Nell Benjamin](/wiki/Nell_Benjamin "Nell Benjamin"), who herself wrote the award-winning play *[The Explorers Club](/wiki/The_Explorers_Club_(play) "The Explorers Club (play)")*
* [John Berman](/wiki/John_Berman "John Berman"), news anchor for [CNN](/wiki/CNN "CNN")
* [Andy Borowitz](/wiki/Andy_Borowitz "Andy Borowitz"), humorist
* [Josh Brener](/wiki/Josh_Brener "Josh Brener"), actor in *[Silicon Valley](/wiki/Silicon_Valley_(TV_series) "Silicon Valley (TV series)")*, *[Maron](/wiki/Maron_(TV_series) "Maron (TV series)")*, and *[The Big Bang Theory](/wiki/The_Big_Bang_Theory "The Big Bang Theory")*, and former Pudding President
* [Francis Cabot](/wiki/Francis_Cabot "Francis Cabot"), American financier, gardener and [horticulturist](/wiki/Horticulturist "Horticulturist")
* [Henry Ives Cobb Jr.](/wiki/Henry_Ives_Cobb_Jr. "Henry Ives Cobb Jr."), artist
* [Edward Everett](/wiki/Edward_Everett "Edward Everett"), politician and orator at Gettysburg
* [Fred Gwynne](/wiki/Fred_Gwynne "Fred Gwynne"), Actor
* [William Randolph Hearst](/wiki/William_Randolph_Hearst "William Randolph Hearst"), newspaper publisher
* [Rashida Jones](/wiki/Rashida_Jones "Rashida Jones"), actress in *[Parks and Recreation](/wiki/Parks_and_Recreation "Parks and Recreation")*, *[The Office](/wiki/The_Office_(U.S._TV_series) "The Office (U.S. TV series)")*, *[Boston Public](/wiki/Boston_Public "Boston Public")*, and the film *[I Love You, Man](/wiki/I_Love_You,_Man "I Love You, Man")*
* [Oliver Wendell Holmes Sr.](/wiki/Oliver_Wendell_Holmes_Sr. "Oliver Wendell Holmes Sr."), physician, poet, and polymath
* [David Javerbaum](/wiki/David_Javerbaum "David Javerbaum"), playwright who has won 13 [Emmy Awards](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award"), including 11 as head writer for *[The Daily Show](/wiki/The_Daily_Show "The Daily Show")*
* [Jack Lemmon](/wiki/Jack_Lemmon "Jack Lemmon"), [Oscar](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") winner
* [Alan Jay Lerner](/wiki/Alan_Jay_Lerner "Alan Jay Lerner") lyricist and librettist notable for collaboration with Frederick Loewe
* [J. P. Morgan Jr.](/wiki/J._P._Morgan_Jr. "J. P. Morgan Jr."), financier
* [Dean Norris](/wiki/Dean_Norris "Dean Norris"), actor in *[Breaking Bad](/wiki/Breaking_Bad "Breaking Bad")* and *[Under the Dome](/wiki/Under_the_Dome_(TV_series) "Under the Dome (TV series)")*[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Mark O'Donnell](/wiki/Mark_O%27Donnell "Mark O'Donnell"), librettist who won a [Tony Award](/wiki/Tony_Award "Tony Award") in 2003 for co-authoring the book for *[Hairspray](/wiki/Hairspray_(musical) "Hairspray (musical)")*
* [Michael O'Hare](/wiki/Michael_O%27Hare "Michael O'Hare"), Actor
* [Laurence O'Keefe](/wiki/Laurence_O%27Keefe_(composer) "Laurence O'Keefe (composer)"), actor and composer, wrote the music and lyrics for the Off-Broadway shows *[Bat Boy: The Musical](/wiki/Bat_Boy:_The_Musical "Bat Boy: The Musical")* and *[Heathers: The Musical](/wiki/Heathers:_The_Musical "Heathers: The Musical")*
* [Mark O'Keefe](/wiki/Mark_O%27Keefe_(screenwriter) "Mark O'Keefe (screenwriter)"), co-wrote and co-produced the movies *[Bruce Almighty](/wiki/Bruce_Almighty "Bruce Almighty")* and *[Click](/wiki/Click_(2006_film) "Click (2006 film)")*
* [Alexandra Petri](/wiki/Alexandra_Petri "Alexandra Petri")
* [Joe Raposo](/wiki/Joe_Raposo "Joe Raposo"), composer, songwriter, pianist; for stage, screen, television [Sesame Street](/wiki/Sesame_Street "Sesame Street") etc.
* [Mo Rocca](/wiki/Mo_Rocca "Mo Rocca"), comedian and former Pudding librettist and president
* [Franklin Delano Roosevelt](/wiki/Franklin_Delano_Roosevelt "Franklin Delano Roosevelt"), US President
* [Theodore Roosevelt](/wiki/Theodore_Roosevelt "Theodore Roosevelt"), US President
* [Felisa Vanoff](/wiki/Felisa_Vanoff "Felisa Vanoff") (1925–2014), who became the company's first female choreographer in 1948 (although women had been included as costumers for many years prior)[[5]](#cite_note-auto-5)[[6]](#cite_note-auto1-6)
* [William Weld](/wiki/William_Weld "William Weld"), former [Governor of Massachusetts](/wiki/Governor_of_Massachusetts "Governor of Massachusetts")[[3]](#cite_note-history-3)
* [Fairfax Henry Wheelan](/wiki/Fairfax_Henry_Wheelan "Fairfax Henry Wheelan"), American businessman, philanthropist, and political reformer.
* [Owen Wister](/wiki/Owen_Wister "Owen Wister"), novelist and biographer[[3]](#cite_note-history-3)

## Origins of name

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Origins of name")]

The Hasty Pudding name comes from a colonial era (originally British) dish called [hasty pudding](/wiki/Hasty_pudding "Hasty pudding"), a kind of porridge made from cornmeal with molasses, honey or other ingredients. It is not clear whether the dish was originally a staple or a dessert, but it is now served for dessert at the banquets thrown by the Pudding, such as opening night celebrations and the annual "roasts" for their Man and Woman of the Year.

## Honorary awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Honorary awards")]

Main article: [Hasty Pudding Man of the Year](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Man_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Man of the Year")

Main article: [Hasty Pudding Woman of the Year](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Woman_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Woman of the Year")

The society is notable for their annual selection of famous entertainers as [Woman of the Year](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Woman_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Woman of the Year") since 1951 and [Man of the Year](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Man_of_the_Year "Hasty Pudding Man of the Year") since 1967. These awards are usually treated with great seriousness by the honorees. Since the unanticipated personal appearance of [Jane Fonda](/wiki/Jane_Fonda "Jane Fonda") to accept her award in 1961, the honorees always attend the awards ceremony, and are treated to a celebratory "[roast](/wiki/Roast_(comedy) "Roast (comedy)")", and a parade.

## Symbols

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Symbols")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals "Special:EditPage/Hasty Pudding Theatricals") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(January 2019)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The Hasty Pudding Theatricals has adopted many symbols. It has two official logos: the first is a sphinx holding a pudding pot, and the second is a pudding pot depicted hanging over a fire.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Its main colors are deep blue, crimson, green and yellow. The shade of yellow used by the club is an ode to the color of traditional hasty pudding. The Hasty Pudding Theatricals, [Hasty Pudding Club](/wiki/Hasty_Pudding_Club "Hasty Pudding Club"), and [Harvard Krokodiloes](/wiki/The_Harvard_Krokodiloes "The Harvard Krokodiloes") are all organizations of the Hasty Pudding Institute of 1770 and share the same meeting space and social events on Harvard's campus.

*Concordia Discors* is the official motto of the Hasty Pudding, literally meaning: Discordant Harmony, or organized chaos, in English.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hasty_Pudding_Theatricals&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: External links")]
